PicoCTF - b00tl3gRSA2

Description

In RSA d is a lot bigger than e, why don’t we use d to encrypt instead of e? Connect with nc jupiter.challenges.picoctf.org 57464.

Solution

In RSA, the private key \(d\) is usually much larger than the public exponent \(e\). However, in this challenge, \(d\) was used as \(e\), meaning \(e\) is big and therefore \(d\) might be small. This makes the encryption potentially vulnerable to the Wiener’s attack, which can recover the private key when it is small.

Wiener’s attack is well-known and already implemented in several python libraries, so we can just apply one of these existing tools.

import owiener
from Crypto.Util.number import long_to_bytes

c = 115086437502275731673272097781697762424392984743937085313078907983141708534103127209061369422244624710244051487609056354854279180803765210603059398466139896106806183627725404974091041324290642399575176117824179215473158649608757236824598749954324879813755570029091383167379392694127331654330900034170769881429
n = 131332497757481949001912139543465410618302634030569548278243917442256517768147577953845420092874770067695602820590985877126339119007518179920146357049778187439203826304409603211244601711124539517278276345812542108300809574100878083986901508882766844234114983629014142658759541772233371632758622743633888468261
e = 33538011847799226368250020101613395671268335186774065947246444028771611171822296788616460177828587696308247384003093514191775813597049365383578275349574856339271575513967618970507948335449386684251564826424403615793131240532516679213070550773104260675755739828900979568267163432422839623503035442715937722369

d = owiener.attack(e,n)
flag = long_to_bytes(pow(c,d,n)).decode()
print(flag)
